title: TD613 Aperture / Flow-Core / Dome-World / Ash Roadmap
status: working roadmap derived from current v3.0-alpha and Dome-World v0.5.0
governing_laws: Open Field governs idea generation; review begins only at operator-requested promotion.
Artifact, context, and relation are separate strata.
Receipts may cross stations; authority does not.
The human custodian remains the decision point for consequential Ash actions.
Proof is limited to propositions whose truth conditions formally close.
Prediction remains research-only until calibration, abstention, drift, and harm tests are satisfied.
No new claim-ceiling mechanism is introduced by this roadmap.

current_seams.name: Local hashing promise exceeds current implementation
current_seams.detail: Ash UI states browser-local SHA-256, but buildPayload still accepts a manual hash and substitutes sha256:manual-placeholder.

current_seams.name: Artifact digest and metadata digest are conflated
current_seams.detail: The server synthesizes content_hash from source locator and metadata when no digest is supplied.

current_seams.name: Reciprocal bridge exists in Aperture but not yet in the server API
current_seams.detail: The deployed API aperture-bridge operation still returns one-way modeled route weather.

current_seams.name: Cinder fragment crosses the server boundary
current_seams.detail: ash-cinder accepts a plaintext fragment field; the raw-content denylist does not classify fragment as raw content.

current_seams.name: Legacy claim-ceiling vocabulary remains in Dome/Ash
current_seams.detail: Aperture's new reciprocal additions removed claim-ceiling machinery, but the existing Dome/Ash API still emits claimCeiling fields. This is frozen for separate review, not expanded by the roadmap.

current_seams.name: Current version identity is split across surfaces
current_seams.detail: Aperture is v3.0-alpha, Dome-World is v0.5.0, Ash is v0.6, and exact substrate receipts remain v0.4.3. Compatibility must be explicit rather than inferred.

phases.name: Baseline Freeze and Contract Ledger
phases.purpose: Freeze the current stable instrument and make every existing station contract inspectable before semantic changes.
deliverables: Current-version compatibility matrix for Aperture, Dome-World, Ash, Phason, and exact substrate.
Machine-readable endpoint and schema inventory.
No-new-claim-ceiling freeze for roadmap work.
Regression fixtures for current UI load, identity stability, animation clocks, and drawer affordances.
Deployment probe script for readiness, ping, and bounded POST smoke tests.
exit_tests: Current Aperture file loads without flicker or microtask starvation.
Every existing endpoint is inventoried with request, response, and side-effect posture.
No inherited module can rewrite current instrument identity.
No roadmap phase begins from undocumented behavior.
forbidden_shortcuts: No broad refactor before baseline fixtures exist.
No version-string harmonization that erases lineage.
No automatic removal or hardening of legacy boundary vocabulary.

phases.name: Ash Local Commitment Kernel
phases.purpose: Create a true browser-local L1 artifact digest and remove the synthetic hash path.
deliverables: app/dome-world/ash/local-commitment.js using browser Web Crypto SHA-256.
File-picker wiring that computes the digest from exact selected bytes.
Explicit L0_METADATA_ONLY and L1_BROWSER_LOCAL_ARTIFACT_DIGEST postures.
Finite Phase-1 file-size ceiling with visible failure.
Best-effort buffer overwrite without memory-erasure claims.
No network call from the commitment module.
exit_tests: Known fixture hashes match external SHA-256 tools.
One-byte mutation changes the digest.
Empty files hash correctly.
Unicode text is hashed as exact bytes without normalization.
No raw bytes appear in any request envelope.
No file selection yields artifact_digest=null, never a placeholder or metadata substitute.
Oversized files fail closed and visibly.
forbidden_shortcuts: No metadata-derived artifact digest.
No claim of immediate or guaranteed memory erasure.
No terabyte-readiness claim through file.arrayBuffer().

phases.name: Canonical Digest and Receipt Spine
phases.purpose: Separate byte identity, manifest identity, and receipt identity so later proofs and relations have stable objects.
deliverables: artifact_digest for exact bytes.
manifest_digest for canonicalized metadata and routing posture.
receipt_digest for the final receipt envelope.
Canonical JSON specification and test vectors.
Schema migration from custody-manifest/v0.5 without silently rewriting older receipts.
Assurance classes L0 through L1 implemented; higher classes reserved.
exit_tests: Changing metadata leaves artifact_digest unchanged but changes manifest_digest.
Changing the receipt envelope changes receipt_digest.
Canonicalization is deterministic across browser and Python.
Legacy receipts replay without being misrepresented as L1.
The server rejects malformed L1 digests.
forbidden_shortcuts: No universal stable public digest by default.
No signature, identity, authorship, possession, or time claim from a digest alone.

phases.name: Flow-Core Context Instrumentation
phases.purpose: Make context receipts sensor-named, source-typed, uncertainty-bearing, and calibratable before reciprocity becomes operational.
deliverables: Context receipt schema with source_status, sensor_id, transformation_history, missingness, uncertainty, and alternatives.
Sensor registry for operator declarations, browser performance, HTTP logs, retrieval comparisons, repository changes, simulated fixtures, and derived formulas.
Benign outage, drift, latency, and noise fixtures.
Abstention state for insufficient context.
No artifact digest field in Flow-Core context.
exit_tests: Every metric names its source status and sensor.
Simulated data cannot present as observed.
Unknown sensor routes to UNRESOLVED.
Benign failures do not automatically become surveillance or suppression findings.
Context receipts remain private by default.
forbidden_shortcuts: No total-surveillance-grid claim.
No weather-as-prediction claim.
No high-entropy-as-automatic-camouflage doctrine.

phases.name: Reciprocal Receipt Bridge
phases.purpose: Bring the Aperture v3 reciprocal architecture into the Dome-World API without transferring authority.
deliverables: Aperture diagnostic receipt endpoint or operation.
Flow-Core context translation operation.
Returned-context audit operation.

Round-trip receipt containing received, rejected, transformed, produced, missingness, uncertainty, alternatives, cannot_establish, authority_class, source_status, sensor_id, and recommendation_not_command.
Contradiction and provenance checks.
Open Field posture preserved outside promotion routes.
exit_tests: A complete round trip can be replayed from receipts.
Flow-Core cannot write Aperture doctrine.
Aperture cannot execute Ash actions.
Artifact references inside context receipts are rejected.
Missing sensor identity is held for repair.
Returned recommendations never mutate custody.
forbidden_shortcuts: No closed-loop autonomous control.
No content scanner applied to Open Field work.
No reciprocal authority inferred from reciprocal communication.

phases.name: Relation Envelope and Phason Continuity
phases.purpose: Permit a custodian to associate artifact and context receipts without fusing them or exposing a universal tracking handle.
deliverables: Local-only Relation Envelope schema.
Route-scoped Ash reference derived with HMAC over artifact_digest and context nonce.
Binding purpose, operator approval, visibility, creation time posture, and withdrawal/supersession fields.
Phason events for relation created, revised, withdrawn, and superseded.
Nonce and key lifecycle specification.
Linkability threat model.
exit_tests: Stable artifact digest is absent from the envelope.
Two contexts produce different route-scoped references for the same artifact.
Withdrawing a relation does not alter the underlying artifact or context receipt.
Phason records relation change without claiming co-occurrence, identity, location, or causation.
No relation finalizes without an explicit human action.
forbidden_shortcuts: No H(artifact_digest || weather || timestamp) composite foundation.
No relation-as-cooccurrence-proof.
No automatic relation creation from receipt proximity.

phases.name: Human-Gated Ash Derivatives
phases.purpose: Rebuild Veil, Cinder, Compare, Recall, and export around explicit human decisions and safer data boundaries.
deliverables: Human Confirmation Gate shared by Cinder, Veil publication, Recall, export, and relation finalization.
Client-side Cinder construction or destination-bound encryption before any server transit.
Server raw-content denylist expanded to cover fragments and aliases.
Veil remains a structural surrogate, not a summary.
Recall remains a non-reliance and supersession notice, not erasure.
Leak Challenge remains a declared heuristic risk model.
exit_tests: Plaintext Cinder fragments never appear in server requests, logs, or receipts.
Confirmation, authorization, and execution are distinct receipt events.
Recall cannot claim deletion of copies already observed.
Leak Challenge false positives and benign controls are documented.
No modeled pressure triggers action without the human gate.
forbidden_shortcuts: No Burn Notice erasure fantasy.
No anonymity certification.
No automatic Cinder construction or export.

phases.name: Independent Provenance Adapters
phases.purpose: Add optional evidence of signing, ordering, and inclusion while preserving each adapter's trust assumptions.
deliverables: Key-holder signature adapter.
RFC 3161 timestamp-token adapter.
Git commit or release-tag anchor adapter.
Merkle transparency or append-only inclusion adapter.
Independent witness countersignature adapter.
Assurance ladder L2-L5 with separate disclosure and trust notes.
exit_tests: Each adapter verifies independently.
Failure of one adapter does not invalidate the base L1 commitment.
Receipts distinguish local clock, trusted timestamp, ordering evidence, and independent witness.
No adapter claims exact-second possession unless its actual evidence supports that proposition.
Signatures identify keys, not people, authors, or truth.
forbidden_shortcuts: No RFC 3161 equals decentralization claim.
No ledger anchor equals exact time claim.
No signature equals authorship or identity claim.

phases.name: Advanced Privacy Research Lane
phases.purpose: Keep ambitious cryptographic and statistical research alive in Open Field while promoting only narrow, testable mechanisms.
deliverables: Predicate registry for narrow zero-knowledge proofs.
Canonical parser and circuit-version commitments.
Differential privacy experiments limited to defined aggregate queries with sensitivity and epsilon accounting.
Threshold future-access prototypes with explicit non-erasure boundary.
Threat models, auxiliary-information assumptions, and failure demonstrations.
Research-only UI posture.
exit_tests: Every ZK proof states public inputs, witness, predicate, parser, and circuit version.
DP mechanisms define neighboring datasets and sensitivity.
Unique Cinders are not marketed as differentially private by default.
Threshold controls can deny future mediated decryption but never claim to erase observed plaintext.
Research modules cannot route into operational Ash actions.
forbidden_shortcuts: No iO theater.
No zero-linkage-probability claim.
No broad real-world truth proof.

phases.name: Validation, Deployment, and Release Discipline
phases.purpose: Earn operational status through reproducible tests, live deployment probes, version alignment, and harm-aware review.
deliverables: Dedicated npm scripts for each roadmap phase.
Python and browser integration tests.
Desktop, iOS, and Android parity tests.
Live readiness and endpoint smoke probes.
Schema compatibility report.
Security review for localStorage, sessionStorage, CORS, logging, and request-size boundaries.
Release receipt naming exactly what is implemented and what remains research-only.
exit_tests: All phase-specific tests pass in CI and a clean browser.
Live readiness matches the repository operation registry.
No hidden endpoint accepts raw artifact bytes.
No current-version UI is rewritten by inherited module code.
Operational and research-only surfaces are visually and machine-readably distinct.
A documented rollback path exists.
forbidden_shortcuts: No release based only on source parsing.
No green animation treated as validation.
No deployment claim without a live probe.

first_implementation_slice.name: Phase 0 + Phase 1
first_implementation_slice.reason: The repo currently promises local SHA-256 while still permitting a synthetic placeholder and metadata-derived fallback. Repairing that seam creates the first trustworthy object on which every later receipt, relation, and proof adapter depends.
